iti83:2.1: “Monks, when a deva is about to pass away from the company of devas, five omens appear:
iti83:2.2:
his garlands wither, his clothes get soiled, sweat comes out of his armpits,
a dullness descends on his body, he no longer delights in his own deva-seat.
iti83:2.3: The devas, knowing from this that ‘This deva-son is about to pass away,’ encourage him with three sayings:
iti83:2.4: ‘Go from here, honorable sir, to a good destination.
iti83:3.1: Having gone to a good destination, gain the gain that is good to gain.
iti83:4.1: Having gained the gain that is good to gain, become well-established.’”
iti83:5.1: When this was said, a certain monk said to the Blessed One,
iti83:5.2: “What, lord, is the devas’ reckoning of going to a good destination?
iti83:6.1: What is their reckoning of the gain that is good to gain?
iti83:7.1: What is their reckoning of becoming well-established?”
iti83:8.1: “The human state, monks, is the devas’ reckoning of going to a good destination.
iti83:9.1: Having become a human being, acquiring conviction in the Dhamma-&-Vinaya taught by the Tathāgata:
iti83:9.2: this is the devas’ reckoning of the gain that is good to gain.
iti83:10.2: this is the devas’ reckoning of becoming well-established.”
iti83:12.1: When a deva passes away from the company of devas
iti83:13.1: ‘Go from here, honorable sir, to a good destination,
iti83:13.4: acquire a conviction unsurpassed in True Dhamma.
iti83:14.1: That conviction of yours in True Dhamma, well-taught,
iti83:16.1: having done with the body what’s skillful,
iti83:16.2: and much that is skillful with speech,
iti83:16.4: with a heart without limit, with no acquisitions,
iti83:17.1: then–having made much of the merit that’s a ground for spontaneously arising [in heaven]
iti83:17.3: establish other mortals in True Dhamma &
iti83:18.2: when they know a deva is passing away–
